rxn_class_summary: Enantioselective electrochemical cobalt-catalyzed aryl C-H activation enabling C-C, C-N, and C-O annulations of carboxylic amides and P-stereogenic phosphinamides with alkenes/alkynes, coupled to cathodic hydrogen evolution (HER).
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of cyclometalated Co(III) species (Co(III)-CPA1 intermediate, complex C1) to higher oxidation states (Co(IV)/Co(III)/Co(II) manifold), enabling oxidation-induced reductive elimination; cathode performs HER releasing H2 as the sole stoichiometric byproduct.

key_intermediates.name: Cyclometalated Co(III) aryl complex C1 (C-H-activated Co(III)-CPA1)
Co(III)-CPA1 chiral phosphate-bound cobalt species (inner-sphere enantio-determining)
Higher-valent Co(IV) intermediate from anodic oxidation
Alkene-inserted Co alkyl intermediate (post migratory insertion)
Co(II) species (post reductive elimination, re-oxidized at anode)

electron_flow: Co(II)/Co(III) precatalyst with chiral phosphoric acid CPA1 performs enantioselective C-H metalation of the amide substrate to give cyclometalated Co(III)-C1; the anode oxidizes this Co(III) intermediate to Co(IV) (after alkene migratory insertion), triggering oxidation-induced C-N/C-O/C-C reductive elimination and releasing Co(II); protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.
rate_determining_step: Electron transfer (anodic oxidation of the Co intermediate) is rate-limiting; C-H cleavage is not rate-determining (kH/kD ≈ 1.0) and migratory insertion is relatively fast (no order dependence on maleimide).

probe_reagents.name: Deuterated benzamide [D]5-1
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD ≈ 1.0 from parallel reactions, indicating C-H cleavage is not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: Isolated Co(III) complex C1
probe_reagents.observation: Stoichiometrically prepared, isolated, and fully characterized; electricity required for productive turnover to product, supporting oxidation-induced reductive elimination

probe_reagents.name: Variable maleimide concentration (kinetic order)
probe_reagents.observation: Logarithmic plot of initial rates showed no marked correlation with maleimide concentration; migratory insertion considered relatively fast

probe_reagents.name: Variable current (product formation monitoring)
probe_reagents.observation: Direct correlation between current and product formation rate; electron transfer identified as rate-determining factor

probe_reagents.name: Nonlinear effect study with scalemic CPA1
probe_reagents.observation: Linear relationship between ee of CPA1 and ee of product 3, ruling out self-association/multiligand catalysts; single chiral acid coordinates Co

probe_reagents.name: Silver carbonate (chemical oxidant control)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave very low enantioselectivity, highlighting electrochemistry's role in enantiocontrol

probe_reagents.name: No-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formation in absence of electricity, confirming electrochemical requirement

probe_reagents.name: Headspace GC analysis
probe_reagents.observation: Molecular H2 detected, confirming HER as the cathodic half-reaction

notes: Authors favor an inner-sphere mechanism in which CPA1 directly coordinates to cobalt (rather than outer-sphere enantioinduction), supported by linear NLE and HRMS/UV-vis detection of cobalt-CPA1 intermediates traversing a Co(III)/Co(IV)/Co(II) manifold. Cathode material can be replaced with stainless steel without loss of efficiency. Faradaic efficiency up to 89% on decagram scale. Natural sunlight can power the cell. DFT used primarily for atropostability (rotational barrier of axially chiral product 36) rather than core mechanism.
